creator: Andrew@FreeBSD.org
text:
I haven't tried using it as a cross compiler. I would expect it to work but this would need to be tested.

The patch is mostly upstreamable, however, by default, gcc assumes armv6 can handle unaligned accesses where we don't support this. The rest of the patch should be able to be upstreamed, there is one minor change that needs to be made to it. I'm planning on testing this change against 4.9 and 5 when I've finished work on 4.8.

The patch is lacking our local changes to build the kernel, e.g. format strings. Other than this I don't see why it would fail, assuming gcc 4.8 could build the kernel.
